articles.title: Zelensky accuses EU allies of 'blackmail' in oil pipeline row
articles.summary: Volodymyr Zelensky says restoring the flow of Russian oil via Ukraine into the EU would be like lifting sanctions on Russia.

articles.title: Ugandan opposition leader tells BBC he fled abroad fearing for his life
articles.summary: He says from an undisclosed location: "It was clear that the regime wanted to eliminate me."

articles.title: Sweden detains captain of Russian shadow fleet ship
articles.summary: The skipper of the Sea Owl One is suspected of producing forged documents when it was boarded by coast guard.

articles.title: Israeli forces kill Palestinian couple and two of their children in occupied West Bank
articles.summary: Mohammed, aged five, and Othman, aged seven, were killed alongside their parents, the Palestinian health ministry says.

articles.title: US names six crew killed in refuelling plane crash in Iraq
articles.summary: The aircraft was on a combat mission as part of ongoing US operations against Iran when it crashed in western Iraq on Thursday.

articles.title: Iran captain latest footballer to drop asylum bid, Iranian state media says
articles.summary: Five of the seven members of the women's football delegation who originally sought to stay in Australia have now chosen to return home.

articles.title: French police detain brothers over 'lethal and antisemitic' plot
articles.summary: A loaded gun, hydrochloric acid and an Islamic State flag were found in their car, prosecutors say.

articles.title: Severe flooding kills 66 in Kenya, as heavy rains continue
articles.summary: Eleven people were rescued overnight after a minibus taxi got stuck in rising floodwaters in Nairobi.

articles.title: Treasure hunter freed from prison after refusing to turn over shipwreck gold
articles.summary: Tommy Thompson spent more than a decade in prison after refusing to disclose the whereabouts of 500 missing gold coins.

articles.title: Cuban protesters ransack Communist office as energy crisis deepens
articles.summary: Discontent is mounting over rolling blackouts and shortages of food, fuel and medicine, exacerbated by a US blockade.
